channel.title: Light Tower Group — NYC CRE Capital Markets Insights
channel.description: Data-driven capital markets analysis and building intelligence for NYC commercial real estate professionals.

item.title: AI Data Centers' 125 GW Gap Forces Utilities and Developers Into a New Capital Calculus
item.description: BofA projects 125 GW of data center load by 2030, but utilities plan only 93 GW of new capacity. The gap reshapes capital allocation for onsite generation, gas engines, and battery storage.

news:news.news:title: AI Data Centers' 125 GW Gap Forces Utilities and Developers Into a New Capital Calculus
news:news.news:keywords: AI data centers, electricity supply gap, onsite generation, natural gas engines, battery storage, utility capacity planning, Bank of America

item.title: The $229M Textile Building Refinancing: Floating-Rate Bridge Debt on a Stabilized Asset Is a Bet on Timing, Not Value
item.description: Walker & Dunlop arranges $228.9M floating-rate bridge loan for 295 Fifth Avenue. The deal reveals how office refinancing requires expensive, short-dated capital even for stabilized assets.

news:news.news:title: The $229M Textile Building Refinancing: Floating-Rate Bridge Debt on a Stabilized Asset Is a Bet on Timing, Not Value
news:news.news:keywords: Walker & Dunlop, 295 Fifth Avenue, Textile Building, office refinancing, floating-rate debt

item.title: Harrison Street's Student Housing Refi Shows Private Credit Is Picking the Right Basis
item.description: Harrison Street provides a loan to refinance VERVE Boise, a 547-bed student housing community near Boise State. The deal reveals how private credit is underwriting stabilized, recently delivered assets with strong lease-up.

news:news.news:title: Harrison Street's Student Housing Refi Shows Private Credit Is Picking the Right Basis
news:news.news:keywords: Harrison Street, student housing, refinancing, private credit, Boise State University, Appian Capital, Subtext

item.title: Brookfield and CPP's $5.2B Industrial Take-Private: A Bet on Basis, Not Just Demand
item.description: Brookfield and CPPIB's $5.2B all-cash bid for LXP Industrial Trust shows institutional capital is buying control, not just exposure. The 12.3% premium and 108-property portfolio reveal a bet on basis, balance sheet, and long-term demand.

news:news.news:title: Brookfield and CPP's $5.2B Industrial Take-Private: A Bet on Basis, Not Just Demand
news:news.news:keywords: Brookfield, CPPIB, LXP Industrial Trust, industrial real estate, take-private, institutional capital, capital markets

item.title: A 45-Room Hotel Sale in Detroit Lakes Reveals Where Capital Is Willing to Go
item.description: Marcus & Millichap sells a 45-room Country Inn & Suites in Detroit Lakes, MN. The deal signals that capital is flowing to secondary markets with defensible cash flow and recent renovation.

news:news.news:title: A 45-Room Hotel Sale in Detroit Lakes Reveals Where Capital Is Willing to Go
news:news.news:keywords: Marcus & Millichap, hotel sale, Detroit Lakes, secondary market, hospitality capital

item.title: Texas Industrial Sale Shows Capital Is Concentrating, Not Flowing
item.description: Partners Real Estate brokers sale of 43,000-SF industrial building in La Marque, Texas. The deal shows capital is concentrating in small, single-tenant assets with clear demand.

news:news.news:title: Texas Industrial Sale Shows Capital Is Concentrating, Not Flowing
news:news.news:keywords: industrial, Texas, La Marque, Partners Real Estate, single-tenant

item.title: PEF Advisors' Quail Run Buy Shows Affordable Housing Capital Is Picking Basis, Not Stories
item.description: PEF Advisors acquires 104-unit Quail Run Apartments in San Leandro, Calif. The deal shows preservation capital is underwriting proven LIHTC assets with existing rent restrictions, not future subsidy promises.

news:news.news:title: PEF Advisors' Quail Run Buy Shows Affordable Housing Capital Is Picking Basis, Not Stories
news:news.news:keywords: affordable housing, LIHTC, preservation, PEF Advisors, San Leandro, multifamily, capital markets

item.title: A $5.2M Industrial Sale in Elk Grove Village Shows the Market Is Pricing Function, Not Location
item.description: Sonica International pays $5.2M for a 39,879-sf industrial building in Elk Grove Village, IL. The deal reveals how user demand and basis discipline are driving small-bay industrial liquidity.

news:news.news:title: A $5.2M Industrial Sale in Elk Grove Village Shows the Market Is Pricing Function, Not Location
news:news.news:keywords: industrial, Chicago, Elk Grove Village, user sale, Sonica International, Lee & Associates

item.title: Northlake Mall Sale Shows Healthcare Is the Only Anchor That Can Reprice a Dead Mall
item.description: Centurion Foundation buys Northlake Mall for $95M, plans Emory Healthcare expansion. The deal shows how medical-anchored repurposing resets basis and unlocks capital.

news:news.news:title: Northlake Mall Sale Shows Healthcare Is the Only Anchor That Can Reprice a Dead Mall
news:news.news:keywords: Northlake Mall, Centurion Foundation, Emory Healthcare, mall repurposing, Atlanta CRE

item.title: Axonic's $72M Park Avenue Loan: The Underwriting Condition That Made It Work
item.description: Axonic Capital arranges $72M floating-rate loan for 430 Park Avenue leasehold. The 99% occupancy and sponsor structure reveal the narrow underwriting conditions for office debt in 2026.

news:news.news:title: Axonic's $72M Park Avenue Loan: The Underwriting Condition That Made It Work
news:news.news:keywords: office refinancing, leasehold interest, Axonic Capital, Midtown Manhattan, floating-rate debt, Ackman-Ziff, Oestreicher Properties, Midwood Investment, Marx Realty

item.title: JLL's $176.6M Student Housing Loan Tests Construction Debt's New Underwriting Margin
item.description: JLL secures $176.6M construction loan for 1,395-bed student housing near UCF. The deal reveals how lenders underwrite construction risk in 2026: narrow basis, strong sponsor, and demand visibility.

news:news.news:title: JLL's $176.6M Student Housing Loan Tests Construction Debt's New Underwriting Margin
news:news.news:keywords: student housing, construction financing, JLL Capital Markets, Beachwold Residential, University of Central Florida

item.title: Brixton Capital's $40.9M Loan Shows Life Companies Are Picking Multifamily Winners
item.description: JLL arranges $40.9M acquisition loan for Allura Las Colinas via a life insurance company. The deal shows life insurers are selectively financing multifamily at the right basis.

news:news.news:title: Brixton Capital's $40.9M Loan Shows Life Companies Are Picking Multifamily Winners
news:news.news:keywords: multifamily, life insurance company lending, acquisition financing, Brixton Capital, JLL, Irving, Texas

item.title: Self-Storage Refi Shows Debt Is Available for the Right Basis, Not the Right Sector
item.description: Affinius Capital and Axonic Capital provide $42.5M for UTEX Storage Partners' two-property Miami self-storage portfolio. The refi shows debt is flowing to supply-constrained submarkets with credible sponsors, not to sectors broadly.

news:news.news:title: Self-Storage Refi Shows Debt Is Available for the Right Basis, Not the Right Sector
news:news.news:keywords: self-storage, Miami, refinancing, Affinius Capital, Axonic Capital, UTEX Storage Partners, Greysteel

item.title: MG Properties' Portland Buy Shows Agency Debt Is Buying Time, Not Momentum
item.description: MG Properties buys 188-unit Portland apartment complex with Freddie Mac financing. The deal shows agency debt is buying time for sponsors, not momentum.

news:news.news:title: MG Properties' Portland Buy Shows Agency Debt Is Buying Time, Not Momentum
news:news.news:keywords: MG Properties, Freddie Mac, Portland, multifamily, agency debt

item.title: Whitehall Plaza Sale Shows Retail Capital Is Chasing Income, Not Stories
item.description: JLL sells Whitehall Plaza, a 365,071 SF shopping center in PA, to Mishorim Gold Group. The deal signals retail capital is flowing to assets with proven income streams and operator upgrades.

news:news.news:title: Whitehall Plaza Sale Shows Retail Capital Is Chasing Income, Not Stories
news:news.news:keywords: retail, shopping center, capital markets, JLL, Mishorim Gold Group, Whitehall Plaza, Lehigh Valley, asset management

item.title: Denver Apartment Sale at 55% Occupancy Shows Basis, Not Demand, Drives Trades
item.description: Marcus & Millichap sells 93-unit Denver apartment at 55% occupancy. The deal reveals how repriced basis, not demand, is unlocking liquidity for multifamily owners.

news:news.news:title: Denver Apartment Sale at 55% Occupancy Shows Basis, Not Demand, Drives Trades
news:news.news:keywords: Denver multifamily, basis reset, liquidity, Marcus & Millichap, occupancy distress

item.title: Hudson Landing's $1B Price Tag Tests Manhattan's Development Math
item.description: Gotham, Fisher Brothers, and MURAL plan 1,127 units at 621 West 45th Street. The $1B price tag and brownfield remediation reveal the capital required to build in Manhattan today.

news:news.news:title: Hudson Landing's $1B Price Tag Tests Manhattan's Development Math
news:news.news:keywords: Hudson Landing, Gotham Organization, Fisher Brothers, MURAL Real Estate Group, FXCollaborative, Hell's Kitchen, Manhattan development, brownfield remediation, affordable housing, Intrepid Museum

item.title: Jadian's $500M WareSpace Bet Shows Capital Is Chasing Operating Control, Not Just Real Estate
item.description: Jadian Capital upsizes WareSpace equity to $500M+, backing a micro-bay industrial platform. The move reveals capital's preference for operating control over passive asset ownership.

news:news.news:title: Jadian's $500M WareSpace Bet Shows Capital Is Chasing Operating Control, Not Just Real Estate
news:news.news:keywords: Jadian Capital, WareSpace, micro-bay industrial, alternative real estate, equity commitment, platform building, small business storage

item.title: Hudson Landing Shows Development Capital Is Back Only for the Right Basis
item.description: Gotham, Fisher Brothers, and MURAL plan a $1B, 1,127-unit development at 621 West 45th Street. The capital story is about basis, remediation, and the return of institutional risk appetite.

news:news.news:title: Hudson Landing Shows Development Capital Is Back Only for the Right Basis
news:news.news:keywords: development, New York City, multifamily, affordable housing, capital markets

item.title: Greystone's $137M Fund Shows Affordable Housing Capital Is Concentrating, Not Flowing
item.description: Greystone closes $137M LIHTC fund for 2,000 affordable units. The capital is concentrating around experienced sponsors and project-based subsidies, not flowing broadly.

news:news.news:title: Greystone's $137M Fund Shows Affordable Housing Capital Is Concentrating, Not Flowing
news:news.news:keywords: Greystone, affordable housing, LIHTC, institutional capital, fundraising

item.title: Ponce Bank's $50M Canarsie Loan: Construction Debt Returns Only for the Right Sponsor
item.description: Ponce Bank lends $50M for Midwood Group's 198-unit Canarsie project. The deal shows construction debt is available only for sponsors with land control, tax abatements, and a defensible basis.

news:news.news:title: Ponce Bank's $50M Canarsie Loan: Construction Debt Returns Only for the Right Sponsor
news:news.news:keywords: construction financing, Ponce Bank, Midwood Group, Brooklyn multifamily, 485-x tax abatement

item.title: Urban Edge's $54M Buy Shows Retail Capital Is Chasing Lease Duration, Not Just Location
item.description: Urban Edge Properties pays $54.3M for The Village at Bridgewater Commons, a 98%-leased retail center with a 7.4-year WALT. The deal signals that retail capital is underwriting lease duration over location alone.

news:news.news:title: Urban Edge's $54M Buy Shows Retail Capital Is Chasing Lease Duration, Not Just Location
news:news.news:keywords: retail, capital markets, Urban Edge Properties, Bridgewater, JLL, lease duration, medical office

item.title: Manhattan Beach Studios Loan Sale: Time Is the Asset Being Traded
item.description: Deutsche Bank and Kennedy Wilson are selling a $240M mortgage on Hackman Capital's Manhattan Beach Studios. The sale reveals how time, not just basis, is the scarce resource in CRE capital markets.

news:news.news:title: Manhattan Beach Studios Loan Sale: Time Is the Asset Being Traded
news:news.news:keywords: Hackman Capital Partners, Manhattan Beach Studios, Deutsche Bank, Kennedy Wilson, loan sale, studio real estate, defense tech, CRE distress, time as cost of capital

item.title: NewPoint's $34.6M Hotel Conversion Loan Tests the Math on Adaptive Reuse
item.description: NewPoint provides $34.6M bridge loan for Clarion Collection hotel-to-multifamily conversion in Arlington, VA. Analysis of the capital stack, basis, and lender underwriting logic.

news:news.news:title: NewPoint's $34.6M Hotel Conversion Loan Tests the Math on Adaptive Reuse
news:news.news:keywords: NewPoint, bridge loan, hotel conversion, multifamily, Arlington, adaptive reuse, Goodhomes Communities

item.title: Life Company Loan on Long Island Industrial Shows Capital Is Chasing Proven Cash Flow, Not Stories
item.description: Northmarq arranges $35M life company loan for 95%-occupied Long Island industrial portfolio. The seven-year term and undisclosed borrower signal capital is flowing to assets with proven cash flow, not speculative upside.

news:news.news:title: Life Company Loan on Long Island Industrial Shows Capital Is Chasing Proven Cash Flow, Not Stories
news:news.news:keywords: industrial, life insurance company, Long Island, acquisition financing, Northmarq

item.title: Charney's $122M Columbia Sale: A 221% Return in Two Years Shows the Value of a Captive Buyer
item.description: Sam Charney sold 99 Claremont Avenue to Columbia University for $122M, a 221% gain on his 2024 purchase. The deal reveals the premium a captive institutional buyer will pay for control.

news:news.news:title: Charney's $122M Columbia Sale: A 221% Return in Two Years Shows the Value of a Captive Buyer
news:news.news:keywords: student housing, Columbia University, Charney Companies, Morningside Heights, institutional demand

item.title: SoHo Ground Lease Deal Tests Whether Development Math Works at 485-x Terms
item.description: Global Holdings and MAG Partners plan 149-unit SoHo rental via ground lease with Trinity Church. 25% permanently affordable under 485-x. The deal tests whether development math works at current costs and rents.

news:news.news:title: SoHo Ground Lease Deal Tests Whether Development Math Works at 485-x Terms
news:news.news:keywords: Global Holdings, MAG Partners, Trinity Church, SoHo, 485-x, ground lease, New York development, affordable housing

item.title: Balbec's $930M Fund Close Shows Private Credit Is Underwriting the CRE Cycle, Not Just Funding It
item.description: Balbec Capital raises $930M for IGCF-VII to target commercial and residential debt. The fund signals private credit's shift from niche to primary CRE lender.

news:news.news:title: Balbec's $930M Fund Close Shows Private Credit Is Underwriting the CRE Cycle, Not Just Funding It
news:news.news:keywords: private credit, Balbec Capital, debt fund, commercial real estate lending, capital markets

item.title: Hudson Bay's $60M Refi Shows Office Capital Is Betting on Repositioning, Not Recovery
item.description: Hudson Bay Capital provides $60M bridge loan for Bell Works Fort Monmouth, a repositioned office campus anchored by Jersey Mike's HQ. The deal signals lender appetite for repositioned suburban office with strong leasing momentum.

news:news.news:title: Hudson Bay's $60M Refi Shows Office Capital Is Betting on Repositioning, Not Recovery
news:news.news:keywords: Hudson Bay Capital, Bell Works Fort Monmouth, Somerset Development, HIG Capital, office refinancing, bridge loan, suburban office, Jersey Mike's

item.title: Extell's $185M Yonkers Refi Shows Private Credit Is Pricing the Suburban Bet
item.description: Extell refinances Hudson Piers phase one with $185M from NewBridge and Meridian, replacing Wells Fargo. The deal reveals private credit's growing role in suburban multifamily.

news:news.news:title: Extell's $185M Yonkers Refi Shows Private Credit Is Pricing the Suburban Bet
news:news.news:keywords: Extell, Yonkers, multifamily, private credit, refinancing

item.title: Maverick's $50M Foreclosure Win Shows Debt Buyers Control the Clock, Not the Asset
item.description: Maverick Real Estate Partners takes control of the Gorham Building via $50M foreclosure sale after a legal battle. The deal shows how debt buyers use legal leverage to force ownership, not just collect interest.

news:news.news:title: Maverick's $50M Foreclosure Win Shows Debt Buyers Control the Clock, Not the Asset
news:news.news:keywords: Maverick Real Estate Partners, Gorham Building, foreclosure, New York City, distressed debt, lender strategy

item.title: Oaktree and IDF's $1.7B Fuel-Cell Deal Shows AI Power Is Becoming a Real Estate Asset Class
item.description: IDF and Oaktree commit $1.7B to Bloom Energy fuel cells for Nebius AI cloud. The deal signals that power infrastructure is becoming a financed real estate asset, not just a utility cost.

news:news.news:title: Oaktree and IDF's $1.7B Fuel-Cell Deal Shows AI Power Is Becoming a Real Estate Asset Class
news:news.news:keywords: AI infrastructure, data center power, project finance, Oaktree, Bloom Energy, behind-the-meter

item.title: KKR's $4.2B EDF Buy: Infrastructure Capital Sets a New Pricing Floor for Renewables
item.description: KKR's $4.2B acquisition of EDF's North American renewables signals infrastructure capital is compressing yields on stabilized assets, creating a new pricing regime for developers and lenders.

news:news.news:title: KKR's $4.2B EDF Buy: Infrastructure Capital Sets a New Pricing Floor for Renewables
news:news.news:keywords: KKR, EDF Renewables, infrastructure capital, renewable energy, capital markets

item.title: Capital Square's $115M Sale: The DST Exit That Proves Liquidity Is for Sponsors, Not Just Assets
item.description: Capital Square sells 2000 West Creek for $115M, delivering 137% total return to DST investors. The deal reveals how sponsor timing, not just asset quality, drives liquidity in multifamily exits.

news:news.news:title: Capital Square's $115M Sale: The DST Exit That Proves Liquidity Is for Sponsors, Not Just Assets
news:news.news:keywords: Capital Square, DST, multifamily, Richmond, 1031 exchange, liquidity, exit strategy

item.title: Intercontinental's $69.5M Buy Shows Grocery-Anchored Retail Still Clears at the Right Basis
item.description: Intercontinental Real Estate acquires Lakeland Town Center for $69.5M. The deal shows how grocery-anchored retail trades on basis defensibility, not yield.

news:news.news:title: Intercontinental's $69.5M Buy Shows Grocery-Anchored Retail Still Clears at the Right Basis
news:news.news:keywords: Intercontinental Real Estate, Lakeland Town Center, grocery-anchored retail, Seattle retail, JLL Capital Markets, retail investment sales, capital markets

item.title: Balbec's $930 Million Fund Shows Private Credit Is Building a Permanent Real Estate Debt Machine
item.description: Balbec Capital raised $930M for mortgage and asset debt, signaling private credit's shift from opportunistic lending to a permanent, securitized real estate debt platform.

news:news.news:title: Balbec's $930 Million Fund Shows Private Credit Is Building a Permanent Real Estate Debt Machine
news:news.news:keywords: private credit, Balbec Capital, real estate debt, commercial mortgage, securitization, non-bank lending

item.title: Affinius's $177M Student Housing Loan Shows Construction Debt Is Open Only for the Right Formula
item.description: Affinius Capital provides $176.6M construction loan for Place at Alafaya near UCF. The deal shows institutional lenders are selective about student housing, sponsor quality, and basis.

news:news.news:title: Affinius's $177M Student Housing Loan Shows Construction Debt Is Open Only for the Right Formula
news:news.news:keywords: Affinius Capital, Beachwold Residential, student housing, construction financing, Orlando, University of Central Florida, JLL

item.title: Catholic Homes' $147M Bronx Loan: Agency Debt Fills Where Private Capital Won't Go
item.description: Catholic Homes secures $146.9M NYS HFA construction loan for 251 affordable units in the Bronx. The deal reveals how nonprofit sponsors and agency debt keep development alive when private capital retreats.

news:news.news:title: Catholic Homes' $147M Bronx Loan: Agency Debt Fills Where Private Capital Won't Go
news:news.news:keywords: affordable housing, construction financing, Bronx development, NYS Housing Finance Agency, Catholic Homes, nonprofit development, agency debt

item.title: Greystar’s $216M Arlington Sale Shows Multifamily Liquidity Is Narrow and Price-Sensitive
item.description: Greystar sells 423-unit Arlington tower to GID for $216M. The $510K/unit price reveals narrow liquidity for stabilized multifamily and the premium on location and vintage.

news:news.news:title: Greystar’s $216M Arlington Sale Shows Multifamily Liquidity Is Narrow and Price-Sensitive
news:news.news:keywords: Greystar, GID, multifamily, Arlington, liquidity

item.title: Citi's $817M CMBS Deal Shows Private Capital Is Underwriting Multifamily Risk Agencies Won't Touch
item.description: Citi priced an $817M all-multifamily CMBS conduit with 123.4% average LTV, the largest single-bank deal since the GFC. The signal: private capital is filling the gap agencies leave at higher leverage.

news:news.news:title: Citi's $817M CMBS Deal Shows Private Capital Is Underwriting Multifamily Risk Agencies Won't Touch
news:news.news:keywords: CMBS, Citigroup, multifamily, conduit lending, leverage

item.title: Zucker's $186M Asbury Park Loan Shows Construction Debt Is Back, But Only for Proven Sponsors
item.description: Ralph Zucker's Inspired by Somerset Development secures $186M construction loan from Madison Realty Capital for Lido Asbury Park condos. The deal signals selective lender appetite for high-end for-sale product.

news:news.news:title: Zucker's $186M Asbury Park Loan Shows Construction Debt Is Back, But Only for Proven Sponsors
news:news.news:keywords: construction loan, Madison Realty Capital, Ralph Zucker, Asbury Park, condo development, Meridian Capital Group

item.title: Arbor Lawsuit Exposes the Risk When a Lender Controls Both the Loan and the Exit
item.description: Arbor Realty Trust sued for allegedly promising refinancing then foreclosing on a Georgia apartment complex. The case reveals the underwriting condition that separates a bridge loan from a trap.

news:news.news:title: Arbor Lawsuit Exposes the Risk When a Lender Controls Both the Loan and the Exit
news:news.news:keywords: Arbor Realty Trust, bridge lending, foreclosure, multifamily, underwriting, lender liability, Georgia apartments

item.title: Bridges Development's $31M Buy Shows the Basis Reset Is Still the Only Liquidity Event
item.description: Bridges Development buys Kingswood Center in Brooklyn for $31M, a 66% discount from 2020. The deal shows how basis reset drives liquidity in retail-office assets.

news:news.news:title: Bridges Development's $31M Buy Shows the Basis Reset Is Still the Only Liquidity Event
news:news.news:keywords: Bridges Development, Kingswood Center, Brooklyn, basis reset, retail-office

item.title: Hawthorne's $90M Bankruptcy Sale: When a Single Bid Reveals the Cost of Illiquidity
item.description: Hawthorne Race Course's $90M stalking horse bid with no competing offers reveals the gap between redevelopment story and market clearing price in CRE bankruptcy sales.

news:news.news:title: Hawthorne's $90M Bankruptcy Sale: When a Single Bid Reveals the Cost of Illiquidity
news:news.news:keywords: bankruptcy sale, Hawthorne Race Course, Stickney Illinois, stalking horse bid, infill development, Hilco Global, CRE liquidity

item.title: Big Banks Cut CRE Loss Reserves: The Office Cycle Is Not Over, It Is Just Priced In
item.description: JPMorgan, Goldman, BofA, Citi, and Wells Fargo cut credit loss provisions in Q2 2026, signaling that the office loan cycle is priced in. But the real test is still coming for smaller lenders.

news:news.news:title: Big Banks Cut CRE Loss Reserves: The Office Cycle Is Not Over, It Is Just Priced In
news:news.news:keywords: commercial real estate, bank earnings, credit loss reserves, office loans, JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, Bank of America, Wells Fargo, Citigroup, AI, data centers

item.title: Peter Fine's $70M South Bronx Sale: A Basis, Not a Thesis
item.description: Peter Fine lists 105 Willis Avenue for $70M. The 419-unit South Bronx rental carries a 3.84% mortgage and tax breaks. The sale reveals liquidity timing, not a market call.

news:news.news:title: Peter Fine's $70M South Bronx Sale: A Basis, Not a Thesis
news:news.news:keywords: Peter Fine, Atlantic Development, South Bronx, affordable housing, Mott Haven

item.title: KPR Centers' $28.2M Buy Shows Retail Liquidity Is Narrow but Real
item.description: JLL arranges $28.2M sale of Westwood Town Center in Rocky River, Ohio. KPR Centers buys a 95%-occupied retail asset anchored by Home Depot and Marc's. The deal reveals where retail capital is flowing.

news:news.news:title: KPR Centers' $28.2M Buy Shows Retail Liquidity Is Narrow but Real
news:news.news:keywords: retail, sale, JLL, KPR Centers, Zeisler Morgan Properties, Cleveland, shopping center, capital markets

item.title: Macklowe's $14M Win Shows Time Is a Cost of Capital, Not Just a Clock
item.description: BankUnited appeals $14M loss to Harry Macklowe. The dispute shows how loan documentation quality becomes leverage when time runs against the lender.

news:news.news:title: Macklowe's $14M Win Shows Time Is a Cost of Capital, Not Just a Clock
news:news.news:keywords: Harry Macklowe, BankUnited, loan dispute, guaranty enforcement, time cost of capital

item.title: Harlem Condo Loan Shows Private Credit Is Betting on Supply Scarcity, Not Demand
item.description: SCALE Lending provides $45M construction loan for 72-unit Harlem condo. The deal signals private credit's bet on supply-constrained for-sale markets, not broad demand.

news:news.news:title: Harlem Condo Loan Shows Private Credit Is Betting on Supply Scarcity, Not Demand
news:news.news:keywords: construction financing, private credit, Harlem condos, SCALE Lending, Slate Property Group

item.title: A $103 Million Refinancing That Is Really a Bet on Tenant Credit Duration
item.description: RXR, Waterfall, and NY Life close $103M recap of Sylvan Corporate Center. The anchor tenant's 20-year lease extension, not the asset class, drives the capital.

news:news.news:title: A $103 Million Refinancing That Is Really a Bet on Tenant Credit Duration
news:news.news:keywords: RXR, Waterfall Asset Management, New York Life, Andalex Group, medical office, recapitalization, Englewood Cliffs, tenant credit
